response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with an impressive career trajectory (Google, Meta), but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure is minimal — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. He has no demonstrable experience in capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or any adjacent financial-systems domain. His core stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js, and his listed skills do not even include C++, SQL Server, or Oracle.
strengths: Strong CI/CD and DevOps background (GitLab, Docker, artifact management) covering an optional desirable skill
Proven communication and knowledge-sharing ability evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and open-source project leadership
Java experience (robotic arms at Altom, mobile orchestration tooling) partially satisfying an optional desirable skill
concerns: No substantive C++ experience — primary language stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js/DLang; C++ does not appear in his skills list
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain exposure; RIDE Capital is a German retail-investment fintech, not a capital-markets firm
No evidence of Windows platform development; profile is predominantly Linux and web-oriented
